text.title: Helm Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Helm Basics`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: `Chart.yaml.version` MUST follow SemVer (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`); pre-release suffixes only for `0.x` charts.
rule.rationale: Without SemVer, downstream consumers cannot pin or reason about upgrade safety.

rule.statement: Charts SHOULD ship `values.schema.json` validating user-supplied values; missing schema is allowed only for trivial single-toggle charts.
rule.rationale: Without schema, typo-class breakage (e.g. `replica` vs `replicas`) silently survives until pod-spec render.

rule.statement: `templates/` MUST NOT hard-code secret values; secrets MUST come from `values.yaml` references (or external secret manager).
rule.rationale: Hard-coded secrets in templates leak through `helm package` artefacts and git history.

rule.statement: If the chart has dependencies, `Chart.lock` MUST be committed alongside `Chart.yaml`.
rule.rationale: Without lockfile, sub-chart upgrades happen silently on `helm dependency update`.

rule.statement: Release names MUST be stable across environments and reproducible from config; ad-hoc `--generate-name` is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc names break GitOps reconciliation and rollback paths.

rule.statement: If `Applies If` preconditions fail OR any `Skip If` trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
